summary: The diff implements both REQ-1 (provenance card/detail view) and REQ-2 (discount code restriction). REQ-1 is addressed via a new NFTDetailModal component with provenance data, transaction history, and consistent open/close behavior across UserDashboard and TrophyRoom. REQ-2 is addressed by removing discountCode from PublicNFTData/PublicORIGYNMetadata types, only returning it in the redemption response (burnNFT), and showing it exclusively in RedemptionConfirmationModal. Overall coverage is strong with only minor verification gaps due to file truncation.

missing_requirements.requirement: The provenance section displays original creator, all prior owners, transfer timestamps, and wallet addresses.
missing_requirements.reason: NFTDetailModal.tsx is truncated and the gallery/HomePage view is not shown opening a detail modal — it is unclear if the gallery (HomePage) wires up NFT card clicks to open the detail modal, since only UserDashboard and TrophyRoom are confirmed to use NFTDetailModal.

hallucinated_features.description: A backend migration module (migration.mo) was added defining old/new data schema types and a migration function, including a PublicORIGYNMetadata type without discountCode.
hallucinated_features.reason: While schema migration supports REQ-2 indirectly, introducing a dedicated migration.mo file was not explicitly requested in the build requirements.

hallucinated_features.description: RedemptionConfirmationModal includes a clipboard copy button with animated feedback for copying the discount code.
hallucinated_features.reason: The copy-to-clipboard feature was not mentioned in the requirements or acceptance criteria — only showing the discount code after redemption was requested.
